schema_notes: Reference implementation for per-billionaire record. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events are canonically scoped by event_role (pledge / payment / grant_out / transfer_in / direct_gift / political / private_investment). Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream, not in this file.

person.wealth_source: Carlyle Group co-founder (1987); private equity carried interest; Declaration Partners family office (~$2B AUM, 2017-); principal owner Baltimore Orioles (2024-)

net_worth.range_note: Forbes mid-2024 profile $3.7B; Celebrity Net Worth Oct 2025 $4.5B; April 2026 Caproasia coverage reiterates $4.5B. Forbes 2026 Real-Time profile not fetched successfully (fetch error) but Rubenstein is NOT on the top-400 Forbes US list tier where net-worth volatility is high. The $3.7B-$4.5B band is tight by billionaire-research standards. Critically: Rubenstein is 1-2 orders of magnitude less wealthy than Ellison/Musk/Zuckerberg — this changes the denominator of every 'giving as % of wealth' calculation.
net_worth.liquidity_notes: Wealth is a mix of carried-interest from decades of Carlyle fund vintages (partially realized, partially accrued), Carlyle public stock (ticker CG — Rubenstein is a major holder but not controlling), Declaration Partners family office ($2B AUM), and the Baltimore Orioles equity ($1.725B deal 2024, Rubenstein leads but group includes Bloomberg, Hill, Ripken — Rubenstein's share likely ~60-70% or ~$1B-$1.2B of the team). Orioles equity is illiquid; CG stock is liquid; carried interest partially liquid over time. Apply 50% liquidity discount — higher than Ellison (25%) because Rubenstein has harvested decades of PE realizations rather than holding a founder's concentrated public stake.

cited_events.recipient: National Archives (long-term loan + $13.5M gift for Rubenstein Gallery)
cited_events.amount_usd_note: Purchase price for 1297 Magna Carta at Sotheby's auction Dec 2007. $19M hammer + fees = $21.3M total. Loaned (not yet donated) to National Archives; Rubenstein retains ownership. A subsequent $13.5M gift in 2011 funded the gallery housing it.
cited_events.dedup_note: CRITICAL: The $21.3M purchase is NOT a charitable gift — it is a private asset purchase that Rubenstein LOANS to the Archives. The $13.5M separate gift for the Rubenstein Gallery IS a charitable gift (captured as separate event rubenstein_archives_gallery_2011). Published 'lifetime giving' totals that include $21.3M are inflating by treating a loaned private asset as a donation.

pledges_and_announcements.status_source: IPS Giving Pledge at 15 (July 2025) specifically names Rubenstein alongside Benioff, Buffett, MacKenzie Scott, and Ted Turner as signatories 'honoring the spirit of the pledge' — in contrast to the ~247/256 living pledgers who have not fulfilled.
pledges_and_announcements.pledge_letter_quote: I had already made arrangements to ensure that a good deal more than half of my resources would have gone to philanthropic purposes.
pledges_and_announcements.pledge_letter_quote_2: I want to see the benefits of my giving [while alive].
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Pledged 'more than half' (standard Giving Pledge minimum). At 2010 wealth of ~$3B, target was ~$1.5B. Current wealth ~$4.5B means target is now ~$2.25B+. Observable direct giving of ~$500M-$600M is ~25-40% of the pledge target — not fulfilled, but materially on the path. IPS 2025 report publicly praises him as one of the few honoring the spirit.

political_giving.substitution_note: Rubenstein is atypical: per ProPublica investigation, he largely AVOIDS direct campaign contributions and instead uses charitable-donor credibility + Capitol Hill relationships to influence tax policy. Direct political-giving dollar totals are therefore misleadingly low and UNDERSTATE his political footprint.
political_giving.observed_total_note: FEC direct-pull not yet run. Per ProPublica 2022 investigation, Rubenstein 'avoided political giving' as a deliberate strategy — his leverage was relationship-based (Library of Congress dinners hosting Congress members) rather than PAC-based. A pure $-count would underestimate his influence on tax policy.

carried_interest_lobbying_pattern.pattern_source: ProPublica 'How Philanthropist David Rubenstein Helped Save a Tax Break Billionaires Love'
carried_interest_lobbying_pattern.lobbying_mechanism: Direct personal meetings with Senate Finance Committee members 2007 and 2010; hosting congressional dinners at Library of Congress (an institution he funds); positioning as 'the perfect good guy' PE lobbyist due to philanthropic reputation
carried_interest_lobbying_pattern.beneficiary_note: The carried-interest loophole has survived every reform attempt since 2010 despite bipartisan criticism. Rubenstein's personal tax savings from this single provision exceeded $50M across 4 years — i.e., the same order of magnitude as his cumulative patriotic-philanthropy gifts to the NPS during the same period.

foundations_active.name: Rubenstein Foundation (David B Rubenstein Et Al Ttee)
foundations_active.attribution_confidence: LOW — trustee name 'David B Rubenstein' may not be David M. Rubenstein (Carlyle). Middle initial and PA location (vs DC/MD) suggest this may be a different Rubenstein family. Needs verification before counting.

detected_vehicles.foundations_note: KEY FINDING: David M. Rubenstein has NO large private foundation in his own name. He gives almost entirely via DIRECT GIFTS — checks written from personal accounts to recipient institutions. This is structurally different from Ellison (LEF at $117M assets), Gates Foundation, Buffett's Berkshire→Gates pipeline, etc. Consequences: (a) no 990-PF payout rate to flag, (b) no officer compensation to flag, (c) no DAF transfers to flag, (d) no assets-vs-grants gap to flag — but also (e) no intermediary warehousing of committed capital — gifts are actually disbursed when announced. This cuts BOTH ways on the Scrooge analysis.
detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No DAF activity identified. Absence-of-evidence only. IPS 2021 finding that 68% of Giving Pledge signers use DAFs does not necessarily apply here given his direct-gift preference.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: Declaration Partners ($2B AUM, 2017-) is Rubenstein's family office / for-profit investment vehicle — NOT a philanthropic LLC.
detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2. Carlyle Group has historical offshore-fund structures that are not personal to Rubenstein.
detected_vehicles.trust_note: No public disclosure of a trust structure holding Rubenstein's wealth. Absence of disclosure is not evidence of absence; UHNW individuals at his wealth level typically use trust structures.

baseball_team_ownership.rubenstein_role: Principal owner / control of Rubenstein-led investor group
baseball_team_ownership.notes: The task brief explicitly flags that this is NOT philanthropy. Included here for completeness / context on capital allocation. Consumes ~$1B+ of Rubenstein's liquid net worth that could otherwise be deployed charitably.

red_flags.evidence: Rubenstein is the canonical case in the Patriotic Millionaires literature (Morris Pearl 2016, Medium essay; Common Dreams 2016) of using high-visibility named monument gifts ($7.5M Washington Monument, $18.5M Lincoln Memorial, $10M Jefferson Memorial, Magna Carta gallery) as reputational cover while lobbying to preserve the carried-interest tax loophole — which personally saved him >$50M across 2011-2014 per ProPublica. Individual gifts are small relative to his wealth (patriotic monument gifts total ~$60-80M — a fraction of a single year's carried-interest savings) but generate disproportionate press attention. The pattern is NOT the absence of giving; it is the ratio of press-per-dollar combined with adjacent tax-policy lobbying that benefits him personally.

Rubenstein's publicly-cited 'philanthropy' regularly includes the purchase prices of historical documents he OWNS and loans to institutions: Magna Carta ($21.3M, 2007), Emancipation Proclamation (~$2M, 2012), Bay Psalm Book ($14.1M, 2013), among others. These are private-asset purchases, not charitable gifts. Including them in 'lifetime giving' inflates his philanthropic total by ~$40M+ while retaining the assets as personal property. Standard charitable-giving accounting excludes loaned assets.

ProPublica documented Rubenstein personally lobbying Congress to preserve the carried-interest tax break in 2007 and 2010; his personal savings exceed $50M across 2011-2014. In the same period, he gave ~$80-100M in patriotic-monument gifts. The net public benefit of the overall pattern is ambiguous: philanthropy flows to monuments AND tax revenue is foregone that would have flowed to general public-good spending.

red_flags_not_present: GIVING_PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED — IPS 2025 explicitly names Rubenstein as honoring the spirit of the pledge
LOW_PAYOUT — no large private foundation to flag
HIGH_OFFICER_COMP — small family foundation has zero-comp officers
NO_OBSERVABLE_GIVING — observable giving of $500M+ is ~15% of net worth, well above the NO_OBSERVABLE_GIVING threshold
POLITICAL_HEAVY (dollar terms) — Rubenstein's direct political giving is unusually light; his leverage is relationship-based

rollup.observable_giving_method: Sum of named direct_gift events EXCLUDING (a) loaned asset purchases (Magna Carta $21.3M, Bay Psalm Book $14.1M, Emancipation Proclamation $2M), and (b) the $5.6M Rubenstein Foundation 990-PF total (low attribution confidence). Captured: Kennedy Center ($111M), Duke ($100M), University of Chicago ($111M), Harvard Kennedy School ($60M), Johns Hopkins ($30M), Monticello ($20M), National Gallery ($20M), Holocaust Museum ($15M), IAS Commons ($20M), MSK ($25M), Smithsonian Zoo ($22M), Archives Gallery ($13.5M), Montpelier ($10M), Mount Vernon ($10M), NMAAHC ($10M), Lincoln Memorial ($18.5M), Jefferson Memorial ($10M), Library of Congress ($10M), Arlington House ($12M), Washington Monument ($7.5M + $3M elevator), Iwo Jima ($5.37M), Renwick ($5.4M). Aggregate: ~$511.77M.

rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.50 × $4.5B = $2.25B; tenure cap saturated (20 years). Expected = $225M. NOTE the much smaller denominator vs Ellison — Rubenstein's expected giving is $225M vs Ellison's $5.69B because Rubenstein is 50x less wealthy.

hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked. Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: Rubenstein's style is the OPPOSITE of anonymous giving — nearly every gift is named. Apply minimal cohort uplift: 0.2% of liquid wealth = 0.002 × $2.25B = $4.5M; widen to $50M for conservative confidence interval covering undisclosed family-office charitable activity.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: Rubenstein is Jewish and has given to Jewish causes ($1.9M Duke Jewish Life, $15M Holocaust Museum, $1.5M Jewish Museum of Maryland) but these are included in observable. No evidence of separate tithing-norm religious giving. Zero uplift.

rollup.tier_rule: Observable giving ($512M) is 227% of the strict 10%-liquid-wealth formal norm ($225M) — well above the ON_TRACK threshold (typically 100%) and the VERIFIED_LOW threshold (<20%). Rubenstein is genuinely giving at scale RELATIVE TO HIS WEALTH. He is ~12% of lifetime net worth deployed, tracking toward 50%+ over remaining lifespan if current pace continues. IPS publicly confirms he honors the spirit of the pledge.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Rubenstein presents a methodology edge case: by raw dollars-given / wealth ratio he is Tier C ('On Track'), but the POLITICAL_PATRIOTIC_PR_PATTERN red flag raises a legitimate question about NET civic contribution when carried-interest lobbying is netted against monument giving. The Scrooge List convention (per CLAUDE.md) is to use raw observable ratios for tier assignment and surface the behavioral pattern via red_flags rather than re-tiering. Readers should see both signals: (a) Rubenstein gives at a higher % of wealth than ~95% of US billionaires, AND (b) his specific pattern of small-high-visibility gifts is the textbook case study for the Patriotic Millionaires critique of elite philanthropy.

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: Giving exceeds expected (gap = 0). Tenure penalty saturates (20 years). Pledge breach = 0 (IPS-confirmed honoring). Red flags = 8 (3 flags × ~2.7 points, below 5pt/flag cap for single-pattern behavioral flags). Low score reflects that by raw math Rubenstein is genuinely giving — the Scrooge List should not treat him as a headline Scrooge even though his public pattern invites critique. DO NOT PUBLISH number — tiers only per PLAN.md.

He is unusually accessible (media-friendly, hosts The David Rubenstein Show) and likely to respond.
